name: vscode-knip
description: Find unused files, dependencies, and exports in JavaScript/TypeScript projects

languageModelTools.name: knip-configure
languageModelTools.userDescription: Set up and optimize Knip configuration
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Run Knip and return configuration hints and issues.

Returns:
- totalIssues: Total number of issues found (0 = clean)
- configurationHints: Ordered suggestions to improve configuration (address these first)
- counters: Per-issue-type counts, plus processed (files analyzed) and total (a size metric, not the issue count — use totalIssues)
- enabledPlugins: Auto-detected plugins per workspace (no knip.json needed for these)
- files: List of unused files
- issues: Detailed issues by type (dependencies, exports, types, etc.)
- configFile: Config file status; exists:false is normal when plugins auto-configure

Options:
- cwd: Working directory (defaults to the process cwd)
- workspace: Scope run to array of workspaces

Iterate: adjust knip.json based on hints, run again until no hints remain and false positives are minimized.

languageModelTools.name: knip-docs
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Get Knip documentation by topic.

If registered resources are unavailable, use this tool.

Available topics (use these IDs):
- getting-started: New to Knip? Start here for installation and first run
- first-cleanup: Guided first pass: read the report top-down and fix one unused file, export, and dependency
- how-knip-works: The mental model: entry files, the module graph, reachability, and why findings cascade. Read to reason about any result
- configuration: Understand configuration basics, defaults, and file locations
- configuring-project-files: READ FIRST for unused files or false positives. Covers entry/project patterns
- handling-issues: How to resolve each issue type: files, dependencies, exports, types, duplicates
- issue-types: Decode knip-run output: every issue type, what it means and its key (files, dependencies, exports, …)
- json-output: Shape of `knip --reporter json` for parsing results in scripts, CI, or tools
- configuration-hints: Decode the configurationHints from knip-run: each hint type and how to resolve it
- monorepos-and-workspaces: Multi-package repo? Configure workspaces and cross-references here
- production-mode: Exclude tests, stories, devDependencies with --production and --strict flags
- rules-and-filters: Focus or mute issue types with --include/--exclude and rules (the right alternative to "ignore")
- compilers: Using .vue, .svelte, .astro, .mdx files? Configure compilers to parse them
- auto-fix: Clean up with --fix: what it removes, related flags, and post-fix steps
- jsdoc-tsdoc-tags: Keep an export but exclude it from the report with @public/@internal and custom tags
- configuration-reference: Complete reference of all config options: entry, project, ignore, plugins, etc.
- cli: All CLI flags, shortcuts, exit codes and environment variables
- plugins-explanation: Config files showing as unused? Understand plugin config vs entry files
- entry-files: Understand how Knip discovers entry files and default patterns per plugin
- plugin-list: Check if a plugin exists for your tool (Jest, Vitest, ESLint, etc.)
- known-issues: Errors or unexpected behavior? Check workarounds for common problems

Can also fetch any doc by path (e.g. "reference/cli" or "guides/troubleshooting").
Use this instead of fetching from https://knip.dev.

topic.description: Topic key (e.g. "configuring-project-files") for curated resources, or path (e.g. "explanations/plugins") for all available docs

knip.deferSession.description: Don't build module graph until explicit start

knip.configFilePath.description: Path to Knip configuration file (relative to workspace root)

knip.cwd.description: Directory to run Knip from (its cwd), relative to the VS Code workspace folder. Use when the folder isn't the Knip project root, e.g. "./typescript", or "../.." for a monorepo root.

knip.nodeRuntimePath.description: Absolute path to the Node.js binary (e.g. /usr/local/bin/node)

knip.editor.exports.codelens.enabled.description: Show CodeLens for imports of exports

knip.editor.dependencies.hover.enabled.description: Show import locations on hover over dependencies in package.json

knip.editor.exports.hover.enabled.description: Show imports for exports on hover

knip.editor.exports.hover.maxSnippets.description: Limit number of snippets per file (-1 = unlimited, 0 = disable)

knip.editor.exports.hover.timeout.description: Limit time to collect hover snippets (in ms)

knip.editor.exports.quickfix.enabled.description: Enable quickfixes for unused exports and members of exported classes and enums

knip.editor.exports.quickfix.jsdocTags.description: JSDoc tags for unused export quick fixes

knip.editor.exports.highlight.dimEnumMem.description: Dim unused enum members (instead of warnings)

knip.editor.exports.highlight.dimClassMe.description: Dim unused class members (instead of warnings)

knip.imports.followCursor.description: Reveal and select the import matching the editor cursor position. Disable to keep the view in sync without it taking focus, e.g. when moved out of the Explorer.

knip.exports.followCursor.description: Reveal and select the export matching the editor cursor position. Disable to keep the view in sync without it taking focus, e.g. when moved out of the Explorer.
